samples.xml_description:
<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate input data in 4 cycles and output the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="IDLE">0</parameter>
        <parameter name="INPUT">1</parameter>
        <parameter name="ACCUMULATE">2</parameter>
        <parameter name="OUTPUT">3</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state" width_description="[1:0]">State register for the accumulator.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="count" width_description="[1:0]">Counter register for input data.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="sum" width_description="[9:0]">Accumulated sum register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The module operates in a state machine fashion with four states: IDLE, INPUT, ACCUMULATE, and OUTPUT.
        - In the IDLE state, the state waits for valid_in to be high to transition to the INPUT state.
        - In the INPUT state, it accumulates input data_in values until the count reaches 3 (2'b11).
        - In the ACCUMULATE state, it adds the accumulated sum to the current data_in.
        - In the OUTPUT state, it sets valid_out high and transitions back to the IDLE state.
    </implementation>
</module>
